task_objective: Implement follow-up code review fixes for unload cancellation cleanup and BrowserView native destroy safety.
design_decisions: Used the implementer skill workflow and kept the change scoped to the reviewed unload and BrowserView destroy paths.
Kept asyncio cancellation semantics by re-raising CancelledError after synchronous unload cleanup runs.
Moved final unload logging into a finally block so successful, failed, and cancelled unload paths all record completion.
Kept SteamClient.BrowserView.Destroy owner-only so the native API never receives the JavaScript adapter object.
results: Baseline validation before edits passed: ./run.sh uv run pytest reported 269 passed and ./run.sh pnpm run typecheck passed.
The new unload cancellation regression test failed before implementation because backend.stop was not called.
The updated BrowserView static test failed before implementation because the native destroy fallback still used browserViewOwner ?? browserView.
Related test files passed after implementation: tests/test_main.py and tests/test_frontend_static.py reported 88 passed.
Full validation passed: ruff check, ruff format, ty, pytest reported 270 passed, pnpm typecheck passed, and pnpm verify passed after rerunning with network access for pnpm audit.
